title: Santander Department

infobox.value: [Juvenal Díaz Mateus](/w/index.php?title=Juvenal_D%C3%ADaz_Mateus&action=edit&redlink=1 "Juvenal Díaz Mateus (page does not exist)") (2024-2028) ([Liberal Party](/wiki/Colombian_Liberal_Party "Colombian Liberal Party"))
0.799[[3]](#cite_note-GlobalDataLab-3) high · [6th of 33](/wiki/List_of_Colombian_departments_by_Human_Development_Index "List of Colombian departments by Human Development Index")

This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Santander_Department "Special:EditPage/Santander Department") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Santander Department"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Santander+Department%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Santander+Department%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Santander+Department%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Santander+Department%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Santander+Department%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Santander+Department%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(April 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*

(Spanish pronunciation: [[santanˈdeɾ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is a [department](/wiki/Departments_of_Colombia "Departments of Colombia") of [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"). Santander inherited the name of one of the nine original states of the [United States of Colombia](/wiki/United_States_of_Colombia "United States of Colombia"). It is located in the central northern part of the country, borders the [Magdalena River](/wiki/Magdalena_River "Magdalena River") to the east, [Boyacá](/wiki/Boyac%C3%A1_Department "Boyacá Department") to the south and southeast, the [Norte de Santander Department](/wiki/Norte_de_Santander_Department "Norte de Santander Department") to the northeast, the [Cesar Department](/wiki/Cesar_Department "Cesar Department") to the north, the [Bolivar](/wiki/Bolivar_Department "Bolivar Department") and [Antioquia Departments](/wiki/Antioquia_Department "Antioquia Department") to the west. Its capital is the city of [Bucaramanga](/wiki/Bucaramanga "Bucaramanga").

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Santander+Department%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Santander+Department%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Santander+Department%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Santander+Department%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(April 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Santander** (Spanish pronunciation: [[santanˈdeɾ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is a [department](/wiki/Departments_of_Colombia "Departments of Colombia") of [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"). Santander inherited the name of one of the nine original states of the [United States of Colombia](/wiki/United_States_of_Colombia "United States of Colombia"). It is located in the central northern part of the country, borders the [Magdalena River](/wiki/Magdalena_River "Magdalena River") to the east, [Boyacá](/wiki/Boyac%C3%A1_Department "Boyacá Department") to the south and southeast, the [Norte de Santander Department](/wiki/Norte_de_Santander_Department "Norte de Santander Department") to the northeast, the [Cesar Department](/wiki/Cesar_Department "Cesar Department") to the north, the [Bolivar](/wiki/Bolivar_Department "Bolivar Department") and [Antioquia Departments](/wiki/Antioquia_Department "Antioquia Department") to the west. Its capital is the city of [Bucaramanga](/wiki/Bucaramanga "Bucaramanga").

### Pre-Columbian era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santander_Department&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pre-Columbian era")]

Prior to the arrival of the [Spaniards](/wiki/Spanish_people "Spanish people"), the territory now known as Santander was inhabited by [Amerindian](/wiki/Amerindian "Amerindian") ethnic groups: [Muisca](/wiki/Muisca_people "Muisca people"), [Chitareros](/wiki/Chitarero_people "Chitarero people"), [Laches](/wiki/Lache_people "Lache people"), [Yariguí](/wiki/Yarigu%C3%AD "Yariguí"), [Opón](/w/index.php?title=Op%C3%B3n&action=edit&redlink=1 "Opón (page does not exist)"), [Carare](/w/index.php?title=Carare&action=edit&redlink=1 "Carare (page does not exist)") and [Guanes](/wiki/Guane_people "Guane people").

Their political and social structure was based on *cacicazgos*, a federation of tribes led by a *[cacique](/wiki/Cacique "Cacique")*, with different social classes. Their main activity was planting [maize](/wiki/Maize "Maize"), [beans](/wiki/Bean "Bean"), [yuca](/wiki/Cassava "Cassava"), [arracacha](/wiki/Arracacha "Arracacha"), [cotton](/wiki/Cotton "Cotton"), [agave](/wiki/Agave "Agave"), [tobacco](/wiki/Tobacco "Tobacco"), [tomato](/wiki/Tomato "Tomato"), [pineapple](/wiki/Pineapple "Pineapple") and [guava](/wiki/Guava "Guava"), among others. Their agricultural skills were sufficiently developed to take advantage of the different mountainous terrains. The Guanes utilized [terraces](/wiki/Terrace_(agriculture) "Terrace (agriculture)") and an artificial system of [irrigation](/wiki/Irrigation "Irrigation"). They had a knowledge of arts and crafts based on ovens to produce [ceramics](/wiki/Ceramics_(art) "Ceramics (art)"). They had cotton to make clothing and accessories such as hats and bags.

### Colonization

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santander_Department&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Colonization")]

Spanish conqueror [Antonio de Lebrija](/wiki/Antonio_de_Lebrija_(conquistador) "Antonio de Lebrija (conquistador)") led the first expedition through the area in 1529. The area was later invaded c. 1532 by [German](/wiki/German_people "German people") [Ambrosius Ehinger](/wiki/Ambrosius_Ehinger "Ambrosius Ehinger") in a quest to find *[El Dorado](/wiki/El_Dorado "El Dorado")*. This disrupted or destroyed many of the Amerindian villages. Some ethnic groups like the Yariguíes, Opones, and Carares fought the conquerors until they became extinct. Explorer [Gonzalo Jiménez de Quesada](/wiki/Gonzalo_Jim%C3%A9nez_de_Quesada "Gonzalo Jiménez de Quesada") later went to the area in an effort to appease the tribes. The colonization process in the area was started by [Martín Galeano](/wiki/Mart%C3%ADn_Galeano "Martín Galeano") who founded the village of [Vélez](/wiki/V%C3%A9lez,_Santander "Vélez, Santander") on July 3, 1539 and [Pedro de Ursúa](/wiki/Pedro_de_Urs%C3%BAa "Pedro de Ursúa") and [Ortún Velázquez de Velasco](/wiki/Ort%C3%BAn_Vel%C3%A1zquez_de_Velasco "Ortún Velázquez de Velasco") founded the village of [Pamplona](/wiki/Pamplona,_Norte_de_Santander "Pamplona, Norte de Santander") (now part of the [Norte de Santander Department](/wiki/Norte_de_Santander_Department "Norte de Santander Department")) in 1549.

Once the [Amerindian](/wiki/Amerindian "Amerindian") tribes were dominated, the Spanish organized the territory based on [Cabildos](/wiki/Cabildo_(council) "Cabildo (council)") (councils) to maintain the dominance and administer justice in the conquered territory. Amerindians were assimilated and subject to the encomienda regime to work in agriculture, manufacturing goods, and mines. These two villages functioned as centers for the Cabildos' territories. In 1636 the Cabildo of Vélez was transferred to a new jurisdiction centered on the village of [Girón](/wiki/Gir%C3%B3n,_Santander "Girón, Santander"), comprising from the [Sogamoso River](/wiki/Sogamoso_River "Sogamoso River"), and [Río del Oro](/w/index.php?title=R%C3%ADo_del_Oro_River&action=edit&redlink=1 "Río del Oro River (page does not exist)") to the [Magdalena River](/wiki/Magdalena_River "Magdalena River"). The village of [San Gil](/wiki/San_Gil "San Gil") was created in 1689, segregated from the Jurisdiction of Vélez. In 1789 the village of [Socorro](/wiki/Socorro,_Santander "Socorro, Santander") was also segregated from Vélez and they were all put under the mandate of the [Province of Tunja](/wiki/Tunja "Tunja"), a subdivision of the [Viceroyalty of New Granada](/wiki/Viceroyalty_of_New_Granada "Viceroyalty of New Granada"). On July 9, 1795 the *corregimiento* of Vélez – San Gil – Socorro was created due to the unsustainability of the Province of Tunja, and local government was established in the village of Socorro.

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santander_Department&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Culture")]

The department's culture descends from a mix of Spanish and Chibcha influences, particularly in the south where the Muisca controlled territory and in the [Chicamocha Canyon](/wiki/Chicamocha_Canyon "Chicamocha Canyon") where the [Guane](/wiki/Guane_people "Guane people") And where the municipality of [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan") is also located, known locally for having around 200 inhabitants are situated. During the colony and independence war times, people from Santander were especially recognized for their bravery in battle and their policy of "not even a step back". Soldiers from Santander were valued and respected but also difficult to control as they were, in general, more politically aware than people from other regions and therefore prone to question orders and law.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Among the most outstanding and representative artists of the Santander Department is [Oscar Rodríguez Naranjo](/wiki/Oscar_Rodr%C3%ADguez_Naranjo "Oscar Rodríguez Naranjo").

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santander_Department&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Notable people")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/8/8c/Antonia_Santos_cropped.jpg)](/wiki/File:Antonia_Santos_cropped.jpg)

Antonia Santos

* [Manuela Beltrán](/wiki/Manuela_Beltr%C3%A1n "Manuela Beltrán") (1724–?), [Neogranadine](/wiki/Viceroyalty_of_New_Granada "Viceroyalty of New Granada") woman who organized a peasant revolt against excess taxation in 1780
* [María Antonia Santos Plata](/wiki/Mar%C3%ADa_Antonia_Santos_Plata "María Antonia Santos Plata") (1782 in [Pinchote](/wiki/Pinchote "Pinchote") – 1819 in [Socorro, Santander](/wiki/Socorro,_Santander "Socorro, Santander")), [Neogranadine](/wiki/Viceroyalty_of_New_Granada "Viceroyalty of New Granada") peasant, rebel leader and heroine
* [Geo von Lengerke](/wiki/Geo_von_Lengerke "Geo von Lengerke") (1827−1882), German engineer, merchant and landowner
* [Ofelia Uribe de Acosta](/wiki/Ofelia_Uribe_de_Acosta "Ofelia Uribe de Acosta") (1900 in [Oiba](/wiki/Oiba "Oiba") – 1988 in [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá")), suffragist
* [Oscar Rodríguez Naranjo](/wiki/Oscar_Rodr%C3%ADguez_Naranjo "Oscar Rodríguez Naranjo") (1907–2006), painter
* [José de Jesús Pimiento Rodríguez](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_de_Jes%C3%BAs_Pimiento_Rodr%C3%ADguez "José de Jesús Pimiento Rodríguez") (1919 in [Zapatoca](/wiki/Zapatoca "Zapatoca") – 2019), Prelate of the Catholic Church
* [Virginia Gutiérrez de Pineda](/wiki/Virginia_Guti%C3%A9rrez_de_Pineda "Virginia Gutiérrez de Pineda") (1921 in El Socorro, Santander – 1999 in Bogotá), anthropologist who pioneered work on Colombian family and [medical anthropology](/wiki/Medical_anthropology "Medical anthropology")
* [Reiner Bredemeyer](/wiki/Reiner_Bredemeyer "Reiner Bredemeyer") (1929 in [Vélez, Santander](/wiki/V%C3%A9lez,_Santander "Vélez, Santander") – 1995), German composer
* [Carlos Ardila Lülle](/wiki/Carlos_Ardila_L%C3%BClle "Carlos Ardila Lülle") (1930–2021, born in Bucaramanga), entrepreneur, founded [Organización Ardila Lülle](/wiki/Organizaci%C3%B3n_Ardila_L%C3%BClle "Organización Ardila Lülle")
* [Carlos Prada Sanmiguel](/wiki/Carlos_Prada_Sanmiguel "Carlos Prada Sanmiguel") (1939–2013), Roman Catholic bishop
* [Patricia Ariza](/wiki/Patricia_Ariza "Patricia Ariza") (born 1948 in [Vélez, Santander](/wiki/V%C3%A9lez,_Santander "Vélez, Santander")), poet, playwright and actor
